title: Easy Grilled Corn Recipes for Memorial Day
meta_description: Fire up the grill! Discover the best Memorial Day grilled corn recipes, from street corn to garlic butter, plus the best tools for mess-free eating.
html:
Memorial Day marks the unofficial start of grilling season, and while burgers and hot dogs take center stage, the corn is often the real star of the side dish platter. Whether you prefer a classic buttery char or a bold, spicy kick, having a few go-to memorial day grilled corn recipes in your rotation ensures your cookout is a hit.

The secret to great grilled corn isn't just the heat; it's the preparation and the tools you use to serve it. Nobody wants to fumble with a slippery, butter-soaked cob while trying to hold a conversation. That's why I always keep my Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders (5 Pairs) ready to go. These silicone-handled skewers keep your hands clean and make passing the corn around the patio effortless.

The Golden Standard: Classic Garlic Butter Corn

You can't go wrong with the classics. For this version, I skip the foil and grill the corn directly on the grates to get those beautiful charred kernels that add a smoky depth to the flavor.

Start by making a compound butter. Mix softened unsalted butter with minced garlic, a pinch of kosher salt, and fresh chopped parsley. Grill your corn over medium-high heat for about 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally until charred. The trick is to apply the garlic butter immediately after taking the corn off the grill so it melts deep into the kernels.

Since this recipe is so buttery, this is the perfect time to hand out the Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders. The bright silicone handles are easy for guests to grab, and because they interlock, I can keep them organized on the serving tray without them rolling away.

Mexican Street Corn (Elote) Style

If you want to elevate your memorial day grilled corn recipes, Elote is the way to go. This recipe balances creamy, salty, tangy, and spicy notes in every bite.

Grill your corn until charred. While it's hot, brush each cob with a thin layer of mayonnaise or Mexican crema. Immediately roll the corn in a mixture of crumbled cotija cheese and ancho chili powder. Finish it off with a generous squeeze of fresh lime juice and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.

Because Elote is coated in a creamy sauce, it's even messier than standard buttered corn. Using the Girla Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders Set ensures your guests can enjoy the feast without getting crema all over their fingers.

Sweet & Spicy Honey Chipotle Corn

For those who like a bit of a twist, a honey-chipotle glaze brings a restaurant-quality feel to your backyard BBQ. This flavor profile pairs exceptionally well with grilled chicken or pork ribs.

Whisk together melted butter, a tablespoon of honey, a teaspoon of chipotle powder, and a dash of lime. Brush this glaze on the corn during the last 5 minutes of grilling. The sugars in the honey will caramelize quickly, creating a sticky, sweet, and smoky crust.

Pro Tips for Perfect Grilling

To get consistent results every time, keep these practical tips in mind:

  • Husk or No Husk? Grilling in the husk steams the corn, resulting in a juicier kernel but no char. For a smoky flavor, remove the husks entirely.
  • Preheat Your Grates: Make sure your grill is hot before the corn hits the metal to prevent sticking.
  • Don't Overcook: Corn cooks quickly. Once the kernels turn a bright, opaque yellow and show brown char marks, pull them off.
  • Tidy Storage: Use the interlocking feature of your corn holders to store them in your kitchen drawer; they won't get tangled or lost.

    Serving Your Memorial Day Grilled Corn Recipes

    Presentation matters, even at a casual cookout. I like to lay my corn out on a large wooden platter, garnished with extra lime wedges and fresh herbs. By inserting the Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders into the cobs before bringing them to the table, you create a "grab-and-go" station that keeps the line moving and the guests happy.

    Plus, cleanup is a breeze. When the party is over, these holders are dishwasher safe, meaning you can spend more time relaxing and less time scrubbing utensils in the sink.

    FAQ

    Can I grill corn in the microwave first?

    Yes! If you're short on time, you can partially cook the corn in the microwave to soften the kernels, then finish them on the grill for 2-3 minutes just to get that authentic charred flavor.

    How do I stop corn from sticking to the grill?

    Lightly brush the corn with a neutral oil (like canola or vegetable oil) before placing it on the grates. This creates a barrier that prevents the sugars in the corn from bonding to the metal.

    What is the best substitute for Cotija cheese?

    If you can't find Cotija, Feta is the best substitute due to its salty, crumbly texture. Just be mindful that Feta is slightly tangier than traditional Mexican Cotija.
